mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive momentum in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects a robust economic expansion, which translates into higher corporate earnings and drives positive equity returns. The signal is based on the 3-month change.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS has shown consistent growth over the prior 12 months.
failure_condition: The rank information coefficient between the 3-month change in PAYEMS and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is less than 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) falls below a critical threshold of 4.0%, it signals an overheating labor market, increasing wage-push inflation fears and prompting expectations of monetary tightening, which negatively impacts equity valuations.
evidence_refs: fact:Latest UNRATE is 3.6%, a multi-decade low.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances when the trigger condition (UNRATE < 4.0%) is met.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous occurrence of accelerating inflation (rising CPIAUCSL) and decelerating industrial production (falling INDPRO) indicates stagflationary pressure. This compresses corporate profit margins and raises discount rates, leading to negative equity returns.
failure_condition: A regression model shows the interaction term between CPIAUCSL changes and INDPRO changes is not a statistically significant predictor (p > 0.05) of 60-day S&P 500 returns.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, unexpectedly strong retail sales (RSAFS) growth is interpreted negatively by markets, as it increases the likelihood of further aggressive Fed rate hikes to cool the economy, thus causing a decline in equity prices.
evidence_refs: fact:The Fed has been raising rates over the past year.
failure_condition: In periods where the FEDFUNDS rate is rising, the conditional correlation between monthly RSAFS growth and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is positive.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp deceleration in housing starts (HOUST) is a leading indicator of a broader economic slowdown, as it reflects weakening consumer confidence and reduced investment in a key sector. This leads to downward revisions of future earnings and negative market returns.
failure_condition: The average 60-day S&P 500 return following a trigger event is not statistically significantly negative compared to the unconditional average (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An accelerating rate of inflation, measured by the 3-month change in CPIAUCSL, forces the central bank to maintain a hawkish stance, increasing the discount rate for future cash flows and putting downward pressure on equity prices.
failure_condition: The correlation between the 3-month change in CPIAUCSL and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is positive over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A surprise increase in the monthly CPIAUCSL reading above its 12-month trend causes an immediate, short-term negative reaction in equity markets as algorithms and traders price in a higher probability of imminent Fed tightening.
failure_condition: The average 5-day S&P 500 return following a trigger event is not negative and statistically significant at the 10% level.

mechanism_narrative: Decelerating year-over-year growth in real GDP (GDPC1) signals a fundamental slowdown in the economy. This leads to broad-based downward revisions in corporate earnings expectations, causing negative equity returns over the following quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:Recent GDP prints suggest a potential peak in the growth rate.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between the year-over-year percentage change in GDPC1 and 60-day forward S&P 500 returns is not significantly negative over a full business cycle.
